reviews.review_description: Bth I love the game the ending was sad but also good i think they should make Virtual virtual reality￼ 3 because this game may actually be the funniest game I ever played

The game itself is very humorous and fun. The ￼ mechanics are a bit frustrating but once getting use to it’s a cool concept. Overall it had a few bugs especially during the boss fight glitching out of the mech under the world, consent restarting the fight, I even had to factory reset my headset cause the game just stopped loading!? But, despite my troubles and cries  it’s enjoyable to play and a solid game.

However in my opinion , it lacks the originality and connection that the first game had. The first game introduced a very interesting concept that I really loved, going into different headsets and exploring new things. The narrative was captivating.  while the second one completely drifted of the concept of headsets and the main story of the first game. It feels more like a completely new game rather than a true continuation or “part 2.” I was disappointed that it wasn’t more closely related to the original, even though it’s still a good game on its own.

That said, in the future I would love to see a 3rd game!

If you like games with a great story and world then PLEASE play this. The game does get left on a cliff hanger and the devs have abandoned it but there aren't really and major bugs anymore.overall I had a blast playing and the campaign is plenty long.
Butttt plz can the devs make a next one cuz its so fun i love it 10/10 my fav vr game and the first one

I find it very annoying that everywhere I turn, the floating bot lands directly in my line of sight. It's difficult to enjoy the scenery and interact with things because of this. The jokes seem forced and I find myself just waiting for dialogue to be over. I do enjoy the vibrant art style of this game, and I like the design of the characters. Overall, I can definitely see why this game has lower reviews than the first one.

Edit: PLEASE FORGIVE ME THIS GAME IS AWESOME. 

After finishing this game it I saw that it didn't matter to me if i had to do stuff over again it was fun and i pray please let there be a third game this was amazing experience. :)

The first one was REALLY good and I was hoping the second one would be similar. This game has NOTHING to do with the original game and the only similarities are Chaz’s design being reused.

Is the voice acting are very good the visual style is not bad at all and the story is very unique I loved how it went

But the bad things are there's so many bugs and I don't mean the lavra in game I mean actual bugs
Like I couldn't hold the battery and it's gone or many lines are cut into each other so I would say buy it if it's very cheap but not at the full price I hope to see them make another game the first one was perfect the second I would say it's interesting and I had quite fun
If they added sandbox it might get crazy
i love the 1st and Second game i lov how you're inside of chad and the references frome the 1st game

I was rlly hoping there would be yk a Chaz or smth hunting us down with a lot more of those little headsets being used. It’s good but not like the first version at all. (I lovedwhen butter asked where bee was tho in the buttery headset tho)I first game is definitely my favorite tho THE SIMPLICITY AND STORY IN THE FIRST ONE IS JUST AMAZING( I HAD HORRIBLE STICK DRIFT in 2  BUT I HAD ANOTHER HEADSET AND IT WENT AWAY.)

reviews.review_title: The best game I ever played
reviews.review_description: This game is like the first part but bigger cooler deeper and endolgment!!!! Love it very much and the ghrafics looks just fancy and amazing!!!!!!

This game is much larger than the previous game, however, this is also its greatest flaw, it can be very difficult to figure out where to go, as well as being very buggy, certain progression not happening until I reload and try again, the music levels have unreliable hotbox detection, the flying headset randomly disappearing in the rescue mission as well as pausing completely exiting that particular chapter, and lastly, the game frequently crashing for seemingly no reason. The story and characters are entertaining, but the first game is elegant in it's simplicity.

When I first heard of this game I was exstatic  I loved the first game so I would love this! Then I heard it was only gonna be on the quest and I was sad since I only had the oculus go. Now that I own a quest and can play this game I did exactly that, and wow this game... Is the best vr game of all time I know a hot take but everything about the game in the word of butter "oozes" charm. All the characters are funny amazing written perfectly! But every game has it problem and this game has some. The mech controls are pretty ok but not the best ussally I would use the ball slingshot thing to move. Also this game has some bugs. First bug I ran into  the elevator would go down without me this only happened twice though. Secone bug when I exited pause it put me in an out of bounds area. Last bug I encountered was I died an just kept dieing before I could anything. Beside those bugs this game is absolutely perfect and an amazing ride! Im excited to see what tenderclaws has in store because whatever it is you can count im heading to scottsdale oof I mean my vr headset and playing it
Very good graphics and movement that’s not complicated. This is a must buy and when I first saw mech Chaz and he said “ want to try another headset “ I died with laughter.
The Game is impossible with stick drift and crashes alot

I could talk about this game for days, but sadly no one has the time, so lemme go over some key points:

the no thank you:
-bugs if you hunt for them (but way less than at launch)
-characters’ animations look unnatural at times
-some segments are very tedious, usually because too much combat or backtracking (Tuscan Alley and The Topiary come to mind)
-the main plot doesn’t progress for much of the game so it feels a bit rushed when it wraps up towards the end
-there are times when the humor feels tonally satirical, but also so random that the real world connections are muddled, so it comes off a little.. snotty for lack of a better word (not an issue with VVR1)
-segments where you listen to dialogue with no interaction are occasionally longer than necessary 
-mech platforming takes some getting used to (the game introduces a new locomotion option soon enough though)
-starts off rather slow

Neutral Differences from VVR:
-entirely different set of mechanics, save teleporting, grappling objects, and a repurposed vacuum
-no horror
-less mystery surrounding the story
-more challenging/fast-paced gameplay
-more open-ended levels

the yes please:
-10+ hour campaign for 20 bucks
-Tender Claws’ usual great visual design
-fantastic soundtrack
-contrary to popularity I found the mech controls to be super satisfying after a while
-the platforming sections in particular feel really cinematic from in the mech, with a lot of large-scale moving parts to them
-the weapons are all incredibly unique, and each has multiple uses that allow for creative strategy in combat
-strange mini-games and boss fights constantly switch things up
-well thought out plot and world-building that expand upon the concepts of VVR1
-the characters and their dynamics are well written and made me laugh a few times
-the avatars in the mech feel alive; even though you can’t talk, whenever there’s a conversation going on, there’s usually some nonchalant way you can contribute that they’ll react to
-made with love and small details that you probably won’t even notice 

tldr; This is one of those rare cases of a game that for every egregious thing it does wrong, it’ll also go and do something truly special that you just can’t get out of any other game. Whether you have the patience to put up with that is up to you, but for me personally, it was 100% worth the ride.

reviews.review_title: I LOVE THIS GAME but one thing
reviews.review_description: This game is 1000000000000000000000000/10 but it needs a soundtrack for all of Gavin’s songs!

You can find all of Gavin's songs on our YouTube channel: https://www.youtube.com/@tenderclaws4960

reviews.review_description:
This game is so buggy my whole operating system crashed. The game frozen and my headset started showing a Meta Horizon OS logo and I think its stuck in a boot loop.

And also some voice acting isn't the best, the game is so confusing I just couldn't figure out what to do. One time i got stuck and needed to skip a chapter.

I really loved the original game. It was so good. But the 2 just messed up.

I wanted to like this game but.... I gotta stop lying to myself, this game is a MESS!


UPDATE: the game got my headset so bad. Its now in Android Recovery Mode. I might have to perform a factory reset.
UPDATE 2: Yep the things cooked. The game corrupted my entire system.

I really enjoyed the first game and remember it as a pleasant, fairly polished experience. This, however, is a completely different game. It feels like the developers were on a mission to cram as many different game mechanics into it as possible, but apparently bit off WAY more than they could chew.

It's just one big, buggy, glitchy, stuttering, and confusing mess that I really had to force myself to finish.

The theme and atmosphere are decent and reminiscent of the predecessor, with quirky characters who manage to be funny from time to time.

the game is just a sandbox game but you are limited to only be inside the mech but i cant even get to the point where a character can let me out of the mech or a tool can because most times i crash while playing a minigame and if that happens i just cant load the save file because if i do its just a black screen and i cant open the main menu or do anything, all i can do is hear the background sfx then i have to reset my save which means playing the game again and again and again
i even got a crash that ruined my oculus menu by somehow lagging it?

reviews.review_description:
Glitchy, problematic, and not even a hint of the brilliance that was the first installment.

Because it was a sequel, I gave it WAY more of a chance than I should’ve.

Perhaps it gets better, but even after a half hour, big nope.

I had set it up for a seated experience, which didn’t work at all when I got to the bar.

And the mech was a pain.

Nope.  Not interested in a mech game.  I was looking for the insanity of butter, pinwheel, and the multitudes of realities.

Nope.  Nope.  Nope.

Now let me say, does it follow the pattern of the last game no but holy God it was fun as hell you went on these amazing adventures trying to find the right thing to do It’s really fun controlling Chas and I think the ending is pretty good if you’re not prone to seizures get this game it is amazing and I think everybody would love it The only thing though is they need to fix some bugs but other than that, it’s amazing.￼
The game doesn't even run on my quest3. Just black screen.

developer_response.body:
Hey bigbigpigmeta,

Thanks for leaving a review, we are sorry you are experiencing trouble getting the game to start. First, please confirm that your headset has enough room for the game (1.68 GB required). If you have confirmed you have enough space and are still experiencing issues, then please reach out to support@tenderclaws.com and we'll be happy to help you troubleshoot.

reviews.review_description:
On paper this should be the game for me, and I do absolutely love it, in concept.

However, it is just far too buggy. We’re a few years after release, and even then I keep encountering game breaking bugs. 

It’s far too easy to end up out of bounds, especially with that teleport ball thing. Because of its fairly open nature, you never know for sure where you should be heading. Now this shouldn’t be a problem, but because of how easy it is for you to end up in places you’re not meant to be, I found that I spent more time confused wondering why I couldn’t progress, only to have to reload my save (which are sometimes far too infrequent seeing as you can’t save your progress yourself) and ending up in yet another glitched state.

And the issue with a game like this being buggy is you never know if what’s happening is meant to happen or not. The amount of times I ended up stuck somewhere confused as to what I should do, only to find a walkthrough and see that it’s not an issue on my end but yet another bug not letting me progress. 

Now with that said, the concept is brilliant, the voice acting is superb, and I really just wish that it was more polished, because if it wasn’t for all the bugs this would hands down be one of the best VR experiences out there.

Tried this one when it came out two years ago and gave up due to near-impossible controls for piloting the mech.  I hate to leave a game unfinished, so was looking at this one again and saw developer replies to similarly frustrated players, saying the controls get much better in Chapter 5 once you get the teleport option.  

So, tried again, and kept trying until I finally got to Chapter 5.  (Didn't help that there are still issues with events failing to trigger and requiring starting the checkpoint over, apparently from teleporting past trigger points.)  The teleport is itself really difficult, but doable.  Thought maybe I could get through this after all.

But then, in the very next chapter, you get a sequence that forces you to not just use the mech controls, but to try to use multiple levers at once (some combination of boost, turn, and jump).  I had a nearly impossible time getting one at a time before.  Multiples at once?  Especially when the turn levers are just barely within the upper limits of my reach and I often miss them when trying to grab quickly?  After about 50 deaths in the same short sequence, I give up.  This is one I'll never finish.  

For extra unfortunateness, even with every nausea-prevention setting activated, the mech-piloting sequences still cause pretty significant nausea, limiting the amount of time I can try per sitting.

I absolutely loved the first game, and am a bit of a completionist so have stuck with this second one despite its flaws. I see what they were trying to go for but it feels like such a big project that they let some essential things slide. It had so much potential though! So many implements (like the mech/copter) that if properly worked on would have been really fun, but it always falls flat in essential ways. Its mechanics are extremely janky and tiresome. The game itself is glitchy, i keep phasing through floors and having to repeat whole missions from the start because key elements glitch out and stop working. Some of the dialogue is exhaustingly long and unskippable, some of the levels are very boring and dragged out. It shows some amazing potential though, the art and everything i think if it got condensed into a smaller but more refined game would have been incredible. Also i like Hernandez so i gave my review an extra star for my artichoke buddy.

I wanted to like this game so badly because I fell in love with Virtual Virtual Reality 1. Alas, I am left thoroughly disappointed, for the following reasons:
1) It diverges completely from its predecessor’s quirky, meta, mind-bending format utilizing a vast array of in-game headsets. I played VVR2 for roughly two hours before quitting, and there was only one or two VR headsets involved before you’re thrust into a repetitive landscape of a VR world, which is the sole area you travel through. What made VVR1 so captivating was the diverse array of worlds you could encounter. Getting bored in one, or finding the puzzle within too hard to solve? Simply find another headset. In this game, there is no variety, and no real headset interaction at all. This might as well be any other first person action/adventure game piloting an atrocious ship.
2) The controllers are so difficult to navigate that it is absurd. How developers overlooked this is beyond me. While the initial 30 minutes are deceiving because you have similar, albeit uncomfortably modified, controls as VVR1, in VVR2 you then enter a mech ship that disconnects you from the virtual landscape you’re meant to enjoy. And piloting that mech is beyond frustrating. After only 10 minutes my fingers and arms were sore from having to constantly hold pressure on a combination of buttons while raising and rotating my arms to reach for different controls in an effort to avoid the proximity to other control points. And folks, this was on EASY mode. The game would have been better suited if the mech ship had been introduced later in the game and as a singular chapter to add a challenging plot point.
3) The teleportation option to reduce motion sickness from the modified free movement controls is useless once you’re in the mech. The mech itself uses free movement, and you’re stuck inside it. I don’t often get motion sickness myself so I could maybe push past this if it weren’t for the annoying fact that there is no way to completely align your body at the control pad. You’re always just slightly off center, which makes reaching for certain controls that much more difficult.
All in all, these three points make the game so boring and tedious that it’s not even worth trying to save. The only reasons I have given it two stars is as follows:
1) It’s a graphic masterpiece which fully utilizes the same artistic style as the first game. The tone the few fully immersive portions of the game sets resonates with the same dry, witty weirdness of its predecessor.
2) The same dry, witty comedy is carried over from the first game, which would be amazing if it weren’t for VVR2’s epic failure of a plot line.

My final thought is one of anxiety. I don’t want the creators to give up on the VVR series due to the number of bad reviews, but I also don’t want them to destroy the tone and gaming mechanisms any further than they already have. I feel they were aiming to do something different and revolutionary with VVR2, and it just doesn’t work, because they changed WAY too much. It’s an art and a science both to know what parts of a game format to keep and what to discard. I just hope they release a VVR3 with this in mind, and can somehow throw it back to the nostalgic love most have for VVR1.

That’s all I have to say before I hastily request a refund.

developer_response.body: Hi SeriouslyAnna, thanks for leaving a review! We sorry to hear you didn't enjoy VVR2, but we appreciate your honest feedback. We'll keep working on more weird and exciting experiences in the future, so stay tuned!

reviews.review_description: As someone who had a massive obsession with the first one years ago (Ive completed it hundreds of times), vvr 2 gave me a headache trying to play it. The controls were janky, and premise was weird. The story did not branch off from the first vvr, and i didn't understand anything that was going on, I was disappointed that they didn't build off of activitude and instead start anew. VVR 1 made me feel like an actual part of the story, while in this game, it feels like you're watching a 10-hour sequence of avatars bickering. don't play this game if you loved vvr 1 like i did and are expecting something at the same quality.

developer_response.body: Hi gabi.villa.58511, thanks for leaving a review. We regret that VVR2 didn't live up to your expectations, but we appreciate your honest feedback!

reviews.review_title: Deserves best game of the year.
reviews.review_description: Amazing ending for an even better game. Thank you so much. I hope to see some sort of sequel to this. I just can't let go. I love this game so much and hope to see something new. Thank you so much devs.

developer_response.body: Hi y6ps, thanks for leaving a review! We're glad to hear you enjoyed playing VVR, and we can't wait to bring you more VR experiences in the future!

reviews.review_title: It’s a game that exists and has things
reviews.review_description: You start off by doing something and after that something else happens then when your done with the thing that happened something else occurs then something catatasrophic goes on and people don’t like each other for some reason or another then you have to hit a non person but it’s still a person it’s just not one then the person is your friend after the friendship occurs you have to beat up many more of your friends then you have to beat up the guy who is not your friend to save your friend who you have to beat up and jokes aside if you ever do make a new game I would love to play test it

developer_response.body: Hi chipthedestroyer, thanks for leaving us a review that exists and has words, for our game that exists and has things!

reviews.review_title: I take it back, good game!
reviews.review_description: After seeing the comment that the devs gave me on my pretty mean review, and just thinking about it. I realized I was too harsh on the game and gave it another go, and also reflected on the beginning, my new opinion is… it’s pretty dang good! The teleporting system makes movement easier, I realized the Chaz mouse thing was actually butter, and I laughed out loud. There was more things I liked more that would make this review go on to long. I bet as I go further it will continue getting better! Bugs are still present though. Hasn’t reached the first game’s or under’s highs, but it’s still a great game! Hope the devs notice this review and that I like the game more now. Great job as always guys! 8/10

developer_response.body: Hey A_Real_Gamer, thanks for updating your review! We're happy to hear you're enjoying your second look of VVR2 and hope the adventure brings you many more laughs!

reviews.review_description:
I am 4 chapters in, and wondering whether to continue. It doesn't seem especially buggy, just glitchy (conveniently part of the tender claws aesthetic).

But is the rest of the game going to be this linear and tedious? The original VVR, if I remember correctly, kept things fresh by constantly switching up scenes, and though it was fairly linear it did feel like there was an element of discovery there. The Under Presents was obviously totally different (loved it). This one just feels like I'm on rails watching a slow and not very interesting story play out, interspersed with platformer action with the worst control system I've ever encountered. 

Please tell me it gets better - I really want to support this studio.

developer_response.body: Hi evandagan, thank you for leaving feedback for VVR2! In the middle of chapter 5, you'll receive an upgrade that gives the mech teleportation-like movement. If you're finding mech piloting to be challenging, you may find the movement upgrade to be much easier.

reviews.review_description: The gameplay and the graphics are decent, however I was majorly disappointed by the lack or enough vvr. You never get to openly explore the headsets in the game, and it's all story driven. For me not being able to use that vvr enough was the worst part

reviews.review_title: Who made those controls of the mech
reviews.review_description:
- You have to steer and jump with a mech with the weardest controls I ever experienced. No fun at all. I was one sec before rage quit and refund on the first „run and jump“ mission. Now its to late. :-/
- Only snap turning when on foot :-(
- only english subs (might be no problem without, voice acting is quite ok so far, but still…)

I LOVED VVR1, like got it on 4 different headsets type loved, but VVR2 is very disappointing. The game is very different and lacks the simplicity and gameplay type that made VVR1 so good. It’s also filled with bugs, (seemingly) impossible sections and many other things. You guys at TC were such good developers putting such time and effort into VVR1 and TUP, but this game feels rough, unfinished, and seemingly rushed. I hope after MANY updates and bug fixes I’ll be able to play the full game through again, while also having a good experience. 

Don’t buy unless you’re a TenderClaws fan.

developer_response.body: Hi HWTaro9! Thank you for your feedback. We'll be continuing to update VVR2, so please stay tuned!

reviews.review_title: Way too different from the first game
reviews.review_description: As the titles says it’s nothing like the first game, on its own it’s fine so even looking at it as a new thing it’s not that great, I honestly wouldn’t recommend this game.

The first game was both interesting and fun to play.  I'm not sure about 'interesting' since I quit before the end, but I can say it wasn't fun.  The controls are awful, and the gameplay has far too much combat.

Unfortunately, I realized it wasn't going to get better too late, and my refund request was denied.

reviews.review_title: Fun to play, with walk-through guide
reviews.review_description: It might be frustrating if you try to play it without the walk-through guide, but the guide is easy to find on the web. When you get stuck, you just go to the guide, and it will tell you what to do next. If you like casual, slow paced, easy to play game, with interesting puzzles, fun graphics, and strange quests, this is a pretty good game :-) ￼

developer_response.body: Hi davecotter, thanks for leaving a review! We're glad you enjoyed VVR2!

I’ve played it for 3hours and it was so fun though there is swearing

Pls make a vvr3

developer_response.body: Hi Granger-Family! Thanks for leaving a review, we're glad you enjoyed playing VVR2!

reviews.review_title: Not Flawless but still extremely good
reviews.review_description: The first game was amazing and the sequel is no different! Sure it isn't as funny, you're kind of limited to being in the chaz mech, and the game is still a tad bit buggy (even after the update it still has some problems) but it's amazing. It took a drastic turn from helping AI to escaping the metaverse with a apocalyptic theme to it. And honestly I love the change and the characters are no different. We have some of the old characters Like erm... Chaz (what did you expect?) Hernandez (one of my favorites) and butter along side new characters like Bush and madam (what is madam anyway?) I love seeing how they all get along and interact with each other bring some of the humor back from virtual virtual reality 1. Now I'll talk about the gameplay! Gameplay isn't absolutely the best but it's nice to control Chaz from the inside and even became the mech with a push of a button making combat a lot of fun. I still feel like they could have done better but it's still pretty good I'd recommend if you like virtual virtual reality or if you're looking for a new game to play

developer_response.body: Hi sleepwalker710, thanks for leaving a review! We're glad you've enjoyed hanging out with the Chaz crew as much as we have!

reviews.review_title: Really hard in a good way
reviews.review_description: At the end it it is really hard to climb and if you are a kid do not get this game

reviews.review_title: Great game, a few issues though
reviews.review_description:
Great game, funny humor, and fun gameplay. I enjoyed playing the game but there were a few issues. First of all, the audio bugged out sometimes and the ending was anticlimactic, I think this game would be easily rated 5 stars if these issues were fixed in future updates. 

TLDR: needs a few improvements but would recommend.
Don’t waste your money.

It’s not bad 
But not great

Controlling the Chaz mech is like experiencing what controlling a real mech actually feels like, the controls are hard and I do get stuck at moments but the gameplay is amazing. 

My only issue, is that some of the characters you have to listen to have voices kind of blurred out.
It’s amazing but there are a few glitches but it’s nothing really big kind of but you can just basically restart it that’s it and it’s really good game so get it please￼

I think that the story of the game is creative and great! Although, I do have to admit that there should be more walking around and shouldn’t just drive the mech. The main reason I give it 3 stars is because it has A LOT OF BUGS. I was on like chapter 6 when suddenly my screen went black and I was in like chapter 10. I wanted to get back where I was but in order to get back to exact part I was I would have to restart a whole chapter that took me a while to complete. I don’t like how quiet the peoples dialogue get when you just get a few feet away. I also think the game can be some what frustrating. Again the reason it gets 3 stars is because how cool and creative the story is. Overall, I think it is a decent game that is worth a shot.
never say this game is bad￼

If you like dark humor this is for you!
This is my favorite game on the quest 2 so far! 
By far the best story game on the quest so far! It's a crazy game!

I've played through most of it, and I have only been let down by one thing. You can walk through a wall, then you can teleport through it. So you can skip through all the puzzles and gates. Still, an awesome game!
